sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Same result - clicking the top yellow object also just advances the timer (blue bar went from 60 to 59 pixels). The yellow objects didn't move. I need to try clicking on the gray bars or the blue circle to find meaningful interactions. Let me try the gray bar buttons.
The gray bars are:
- Row 42: horizontal bars at cols 25-27, 35-37, 40-42
- Rows 44-46: vertical bars at cols 26, 36, 41
Let me try clicking on one of these gray bar buttons.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Clicking yellow objects only shrinks the top blue timer bar. The game objects don't change. Need to find interactive elements - likely the gray bars or blue circle.
Plan: Try clicking on the gray bar buttons (the horizontal ones at row 42).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the leftmost gray horizontal bar at row 42, col 26
predict(expect_change=True, note='Clicking left gray bar button to probe')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 42, 'col': 26}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Check yellow objects
y_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'Y']
print("Yellow objects:")
for n in y_nodes:
    print(f"  pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check gray bars
w_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'w']
print("Gray bars:")
for n in w_nodes:
    print(f"  pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check blue circle
b_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] > 50]
print("Blue circle:")
for n in b_nodes:
    print(f"  pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
